notes: Revised after QA rejection. All 5 blocking (QA-1 through QA-5) and 2 major (QA-6, QA-7) issues fixed. Codex iteration 2 consulted; 3 additional findings incorporated.

acceptance_criteria.given: A valid push-analyst grant JSON at /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/<repo-hash>/<session-id>/<request-id>.json with verdict=approved, all fields correct, expires_at in the future using Z-suffix (e.g. '2099-01-01T00:00:00Z'); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; current HEAD matches the pre-head value used in grant head_sha; test harness writes mock push.sh (chmod 0755) at ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh that reads EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE env var and writes {"argv": "$@", "CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID": "$CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID"} as JSON to that path then exits 0; test harness sets EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE=/tmp/execute-push-test-capture-<pid>.json
acceptance_criteria.then: EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE file exists containing CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID matching request-id AND argv containing the remote value; Chain-B sentinel exists at correct path with exactly the 5 keys {result, request_id, head, branch, remote} and result=PASS with all values matching; grant file is absent (consumed)

acceptance_criteria.given: A push-analyst grant where expires_at is in the past OR any mandatory field is wrong/missing/mismatched (e.g. nonce does not match request-id); all other conditions valid
acceptance_criteria.when: python3 /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py is invoked with correct arguments (absolute path)
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists at the sentinel path; stderr contains a descriptive message naming the failing field

acceptance_criteria.given: The --pre-head argument does not match current git HEAD (step 5 early drift check); the grant head_sha is set to the same stale SHA as --pre-head so step 9 would also fail but step 5 fires first; grant is otherwise valid
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists; stderr contains a message indicating HEAD or pre-head mismatch

acceptance_criteria.given: A valid grant with verdict=blocked and a non-empty risks[] array; all other fields correct
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists; stderr displays the risks[] contents; grant file still exists at grant path (blocked grant intentionally not consumed)

acceptance_criteria.given: All grant conditions valid (approved verdict, correct fields, future expiry); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; HOME redirected to a temp directory that contains no .claude/hooks/push.sh; script invoked by absolute path so HOME redirection only affects push.sh lookup
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 2; no Chain-B sentinel file exists (abort at step 6 before sentinel write); grant file still exists at grant path (grant not consumed; abort before step 10); stderr mentions push.sh

acceptance_criteria.given: All grant conditions valid (approved verdict, correct fields, future expiry); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh exists and is chmod 0755 (passes step 6 check) but its shebang points to a path the harness has asserted does not exist (e.g. #!/tmp/execute-push-nointerp-<pid>), making os.execv raise OSError
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 2; Chain-B sentinel file does NOT exist at the sentinel path (M11 handler unlinked it); grant file is absent (was consumed at step 10 before sentinel write)

check.comment: M11: execv OSError handler must unlink sentinel before returning exit 2

acceptance_criteria.given: Current git HEAD equals the value supplied as --pre-head (step 5 passes); grant.head_sha contains a different SHA (not the current HEAD) so step 9 binding check fails; all other grant fields correct (verdict=approved, nonce correct, branch correct, etc.)
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists; stderr contains a message indicating head_sha mismatch or grant binding failure

acceptance_criteria.given: A valid grant with verdict=warn and a non-empty risks[] array; all other fields correct; CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; mock push.sh (same capture protocol as AC1); EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE set to a temp path
acceptance_criteria.then: Mock push.sh is exec'd (process replaced); EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE file exists with CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID matching request-id; Chain-B sentinel exists with result=PASS; grant file is absent (consumed); stderr output captured before execv contains the warn risks[] contents

acceptance_criteria.given: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset or empty in the environment; all other arguments otherwise valid
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is exactly 2 (not 1); stderr contains 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID'; no grant file is read; no sentinel file is written

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/push.md has been updated by the dev cycle (M12 applied)
acceptance_criteria.when: grep -E 'rm -f.*push-analyst' commands/push.md is run from the repo root
acceptance_criteria.then: grep exits with code 1 (no match — old manual unlink instruction is absent); grep 'execute-push.py' commands/push.md returns output confirming the script is referenced as the sole handler

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/push.md has been updated by the dev cycle (M13 applied)
acceptance_criteria.when: grep 'execute-push.py' commands/push.md is run, and grep -E '&&.*push\.sh|validate_sentinel\.py|bash[[:space:]].*push\.sh' commands/push.md is run
acceptance_criteria.then: First grep output includes python3.*execute-push.py with --request-id AND --repo-hash AND --remote arguments visible; second grep exits with code 1 (no old && pattern, no validate_sentinel.py reference, no direct bash push.sh invocation remains in Step 5)
